description: Low-token orientation manifest for agents working with modal-sandbox-sdk.

golden_workflows.purpose: Inspect local readiness before creating Modal resources.
golden_workflows.success_signal: quickstart reports ready_to_run or gives setup next steps.

golden_workflows.purpose: Create one short-lived sandbox and verify command execution.
golden_workflows.success_signal: command JSON has exit_code 0 and expected stdout.

golden_workflows.purpose: Preserve files across separate CLI calls using a Modal workspace volume.
golden_workflows.success_signal: read returns the file content, snapshot names the workspace volume, and sync exits 0.

golden_workflows.purpose: Reuse one live sandbox for iterative work, then terminate it.
golden_workflows.success_signal: start returns sandbox_id and sandbox_name; stop returns status terminated.

name: sandbox-agent-manifest

path_rules.absolute_paths: Used as absolute paths inside the sandbox.
path_rules.workspace_escape: Relative paths using '..' cannot escape the workspace.

planning.active_plan_rule: If active initiatives exist, read their PLAN file and JSON/JSONL state before editing.
planning.completed_plan_rule: Do not read completed plan state unless doing archaeology or release retrospective work.

product_boundary: Small Python SDK and JSON-first CLI for Modal Sandbox workflows.
Not a generic sandbox platform or replacement for Modal's full SDK.
Keep Modal imported lazily and default validation resource-free.

cli_workflows.purpose: Safe discovery, live Modal command choices, volume workflows, and JSON output interpretation.

modal_upstream.purpose: Modal-owned SDK guidance when installed; repo-local skills remain source of truth for this package.

package_maintenance.purpose: SDK, CLI, provider, docs, tests, packaging, and release-facing changes.

repo_understanding.purpose: Repo orientation, product boundaries, golden workflows, and planning state.

understanding_check.purpose: Quiz or coach users on repo architecture, workflows, docs, and validation rules.
